data.title: Gigante Salmon Sets May Annual Meeting as Land-Based Project Advances
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Gigante Salmon AS ( (DE:96M) ) has provided an announcement.

Gigante Salmon AS has called its annual general meeting for 13 May 2026 at its headquarters in Bodø, with formal notice to be distributed to all registered shareholders and documents made available on the company’s website. The meeting underscores the company’s progression as a listed land-based aquaculture player, giving investors a scheduled forum for governance and oversight as it advances construction of its first facility and refines its hybrid flow-through concept in Norway’s salmon industry.

By adhering to regulatory disclosure requirements under the Norwegian Securities Trading Act, Gigante Salmon reinforces its commitment to transparency in a sector facing growing scrutiny over sustainability and fish welfare. The timing of the meeting is likely to focus stakeholder attention on project milestones, financing needs, and operational plans for scaling its land-based model, factors that could influence perceptions of its competitiveness versus conventional sea-based salmon farmers.

More about Gigante Salmon AS

Gigante Salmon AS is a Norwegian land-based salmon farming company developing its first production facility on Lille Indre Rosøy in Rødøy. Its aquaculture concept uses a flow-through system that combines advantages of conventional and land-based salmon farming while seeking to avoid environmental and operational issues seen in traditional sea-based operations.

Current Market Cap: NOK1.91B

European Metals Holdings Limited ( (AU:EMH) ) has shared an announcement.

European Metals Holdings Limited has notified the ASX of its intention to issue up to 3.6 million performance rights, which will expire three years from the date of issue. The proposed securities issue, structured as a placement or other form of issue, is scheduled for 2 June 2026 and reflects the company’s continued use of performance-linked equity to support its corporate and incentive plans.

The announcement formalises the company’s request for quotation of the new class of securities on the ASX, subject to the exchange’s approval and standard listing rules. This move may moderately dilute existing holders but is likely aimed at aligning management and stakeholder incentives with longer-term performance and value creation objectives.

More about European Metals Holdings Limited

European Metals Holdings Limited is an ASX-listed company operating in the resources sector, focused on the exploration and development of mineral assets in Europe. The company’s securities trade under the ticker EMH on the Australian Securities Exchange, and it regularly uses equity-based instruments as part of its capital management and incentive structures.

Celebrus Technologies ( (GB:CLBS) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Celebrus Technologies has repurchased 50,000 of its own ordinary shares at a volume-weighted average price of 85 pence per share, with the stock to be held in treasury, in a move that can signal confidence in the company’s valuation and capital position. Following this transaction, the company now has 40,431,453 shares in issue, of which 1,887,566 are held in treasury, leaving 38,543,887 voting shares outstanding, a key figure for investors monitoring disclosure thresholds under U.K. market rules.

The updated share count and voting rights position may affect how institutional and other shareholders assess their holdings relative to regulatory reporting requirements. The buyback also modestly reduces the free float, potentially impacting liquidity while slightly enhancing earnings and ownership percentages for remaining shareholders, depending on future use of the treasury shares.

Celebrus Technologies plc, listed on AIM under the ticker CLBS, provides first-party data software that helps brands improve customer relationships through real-time behavioural data capture and analytics. data.title: Essentra Cancels 200,000 Shares in Ongoing Buyback Programme
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Essentra ( (GB:ESNT) ) is now available.

Essentra has continued to execute its previously announced share buyback programme, repurchasing 200,000 ordinary shares of 25 pence each on the London Stock Exchange between 22 and 27 April 2026 at volume‑weighted average prices ranging from about 81.6p to 84.4p. The company plans to cancel these shares, which will reduce the number of shares in issue and leave 284,327,744 ordinary shares outstanding (excluding treasury shares), thereby adjusting the total voting rights used by investors for regulatory disclosure thresholds.

The latest transactions, carried out through Peel Hunt LLP, underline Essentra’s ongoing capital management strategy aimed at shrinking its free‑float equity base. The reduction in voting share capital may marginally enhance earnings per share and alter individual shareholders’ percentage holdings, while also clarifying the new denominator investors must use when assessing whether they must notify changes in their stakes under U.K. disclosure rules.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:ESNT) stock is a Buy with a £130.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Essentra stock, see the GB:ESNT Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ESNT is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by weak profitability and deteriorating cash flow trends, alongside a very high P/E multiple. Technical indicators also lean bearish with the price below key moving averages and a negative MACD, while the balance sheet and dividend yield provide only modest support.

To see Spark’s full report on ESNT stock, click here.

More about Essentra

Essentra plc is a U.K.-listed industrial company whose shares trade on the London Stock Exchange. Red Sky Energy has scheduled its 2026 Annual General Meeting to be held online on 28 May 2026 at 9:00 a.m. Melbourne time, with shareholders able to access the meeting and related documents via the company’s website and ASX announcements platform. data.title: Nordic Mining Targets Steady-State Output at Engebø After Challenging First Production Year
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Nordic Mining ASA ( (DE:7NM0) ).

Nordic Mining has published its 2025 annual report, highlighting the first year of production at its Engebø mine, where it produced 150 metric tons of rutile and 12,729 metric tons of garnet but experienced a slower ramp-up due to plant uptime, equipment reliability issues, and bottlenecks in the dry plant. The company has launched an improvement plan for 2026, brought in new operational management and expert partners, and maintains a target of reaching steady-state production at design capacity by the end of 2026, with the board assessing liquidity as sufficient though with limited headroom.

Management sees 2026 as a year of operational consolidation, expecting main performance gains from the second quarter and significant effects in the second half as Engebø moves toward unlocking its long-term earnings potential in markets characterized by tightening supply and rising demand for critical minerals. Beyond Engebø, Nordic Mining plans to further develop its high-purity quartz deposit in Kvinnherad, reinforcing a growth pipeline that could strengthen its strategic position in European critical minerals supply and support long-term shareholder value despite near-term operational and financial constraints.

More about Nordic Mining ASA

Nordic Mining ASA is a Norway-based resource company focused on critical minerals, listed on Euronext Oslo Børs. Through its Engebø Rutile and Garnet operation on Norway’s west coast, the first greenfield mine in the country in four decades, it aims to become one of the few global natural rutile producers and the only garnet producer in Europe, while also advancing a high-purity quartz project in Kvinnherad.

Average Trading Volume: 279,215

Current Market Cap: NOK1.47B

Learn more about 7NM0 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

IDEX Biometrics ( (IDXAF) ) has issued an update.

IDEX Biometrics ASA, a Norwegian fingerprint biometrics specialist focused on secure payment and digital identity cards, has completed a private placement raising approximately NOK 80 million in new equity. From 29 April 2026, its shares trade without rights to a planned subsequent repair offering, clarifying entitlement for existing and new investors.

The transaction strengthens the company’s capital base to support ongoing commercialization of its biometric solutions and may enhance its competitive position in the growing market for secure authentication technologies. The ex-date announcement helps the market price the shares correctly relative to participation in the potential follow-on offering, providing greater transparency for stakeholders.

More about IDEX Biometrics

IDEX Biometrics ASA is a global technology company specializing in fingerprint biometrics, providing authentication solutions across payments, access control and digital identity. The company focuses on card-based applications for secure payments and digital authentication, leveraging patented sensor technologies, integrated circuits and software, and works with leading card manufacturers and technology partners to bring its products to market.
